Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint That Adds Three Defendants And Punitive Damages Claims Against Defendants Dr. Seo And Western Dental Services, Inc
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CRAIG A. KRONER, TEL. N. 1-408-433-9893 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. MICHAEL J. SNOKE OF ZARO & SILLIS, TEL. NO. 1-916-442-4840 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Shanell Williams, Kesha Johnson, and the Estate of Lisa Williams' motion for leave to file a second amended complaint that adds three defendants and punitive damages claims against defendants Dr. Seo and Western Dental Services is granted as to the portion of the motion requesting leave to add the three new defendants and denied as to the portion of the motion requesting leave to allege punitive damages allegations as to Dr. Seo and WDS. California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings and defendants' failure to show legal prejudice are sufficient to allow plaintiffs to add the three new defendants. The arguments that the proposed claims against the new defendants are substantively deficient are more appropriately addressed on demurrer or motion to strike. Plaintiffs have provided insufficient evidence showing that Dr. Seo acted fraudulently, oppressively, or maliciously. Plaintiffs' assertion that Dr. Seo conspired or aided and abetted Dr. Wang is based on speculation rather than admissible evidence. The only evidence proffered as to Dr. Seo's conduct shows he examined Ms. Williams on June 15, 2015 and recommended the tooth extractions performed. This is insufficient to permit punitive damages allegations against Dr. Seo per CCP 425.13. Plaintiffs failed to seek punitive damages against WDS as soon as reasonably practicable as required by Goodstein v. Superior Court (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 1635, 1645. Plaintiffs considered seeking punitive damages against WDS more than eleven months ago. No later than May 22, 2017 plaintiffs may file an amended complaint adding the three new defendants. The amended complaint must also separately state each cause of action and state, as to each cause of action, the defendants who are sued on that cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

The Court confirms the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS SHANELL WILLIAMS, KESHA JOHNSON and THE ESTATE OF LISA DIANA WILLIAMS' Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint is GRANTED on the condition that the first amended complaint explicitly states that the third and fourth causes of action are not alleged against Dr. Mok. Plaintiffs are also granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege any additional facts in their third and fourth causes of action to satisfy the pleading requirements stated in Winn v. Pioneer Medical Group, Inc. (2016) 63 Cal. 4th 148. Plaintiffs have shown good cause for amending their complaint and defendants have not shown that they would be prejudiced by granting this motion. Granting the motion is also supported by California's public policy liberally granting requests to amend pleadings. Any asserted substantive deficiencies in the first amended complaint, including failure to sufficiently allege a claim for violation of the dependent adult abuse and neglect statute, are better addressed by way of a demurrer or motion to strike. Court grantes 30 days leave. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
